description: The German Credit dataset, sourced from UCI and contributed by Dr. Hans Hofmann in 1994, is designed to classify individuals as good or bad credit risks based on a set of attributes. The dataset includes a cost matrix that penalizes misclassification of bad credit risks as good more heavily than the reverse. It is intended for use in credit risk assessment and related financial domains.

attribute_introduction.checking_status: Status of existing checking account, in Deutsche Mark.
attribute_introduction.credit_history: Credit history including credits taken, paid back duly, delays, and critical accounts.
attribute_introduction.purpose: Purpose of the credit, such as car or television.
attribute_introduction.savings_status: Status of savings account/bonds, in Deutsche Mark.
attribute_introduction.employment: Present employment, in number of years.
attribute_introduction.installment_commitment: Installment rate in percentage of disposable income.
attribute_introduction.personal_status: Personal status (married, single, etc.) and sex.
attribute_introduction.other_payment_plans: Other installment plans, such as banks or stores.
attribute_introduction.housing: Housing status, such as rent or own.
attribute_introduction.existing_credits: Number of existing credits at this bank.
attribute_introduction.num_dependents: Number of people being liable to provide maintenance for.
attribute_introduction.class: The target feature indicating whether the individual is a good or bad credit risk.
